currentState: Political shield under open emergency. Lor'themar still buys road for Sylvanas, but chapter 14 reduces the exchange to recall pressure, fear, and the last spendable reason either of them can still offer.
powerDynamic: Sylvanas still owns field priority. Lor'themar owns the procedural battlefield behind her and can translate chamber panic into time bought on the road — though now that time comes as noise rather than votes.

physicalDynamics.pattern: They ride side by side in relaxed formation, sharing silence as comfortably as banter.
The split at the road fork happens with a glance and a few clipped lines, showing how little explanation their trust requires.
The chapter replaces the easy side-by-side patrol image with a roadside intercept: Lor'themar arriving blown hard, Sylvanas already reading the missing buckle and the message before he speaks.
There is no shared physical space in chapter 14, only relay proximity: voice in crystal, hoofbeats maintained, and a refusal delivered as continued southbound motion instead of gesture.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsLorThemar: Answers with stripped operational truth instead of reassurance; the old first-name shorthand remains, but warmth does not.
nameUsage.lorThemarCallsSylvanas: Begins with “Ranger-General,” then drops to “Sylvanas” only when he needs a reason he can spend politically.

history.event: The chapter establishes decades of operational familiarity through shared humor, tactical shorthand, and zero need for ceremonial deference in private.
When the border report lands, they immediately divide labor along the line they already trust: he takes the Convocation, she takes the threat.
Lor'themar intercepts the team in person because distance and relays are no longer enough for the pressure closing around Sylvanas.
He states the real political line aloud and helps force the Barov/Hearthglen split into professional clarity.
Lor'themar delivers the Convocation's formal recall and admits the pressure behind it has intensified.
Sylvanas refuses the recall by continuing south and gives Lor'themar only the argument that Hearthglen nearly failed and the dead are still standing inside the walls.
The exchange closes on “Then buy noise,” which redefines Lor'themar's role from chamber manager to active procedural rearguard.

trajectory: Stable mutual reliance -> battered mutual reliance under converging institutional failure -> chapter 14's emergency political shield, held together by shorthand, fear, and exhausted trust.

stateHistory.currentState: Old shorthand under political collapse. Lor'themar arrives worn enough to prove the shield is failing, and Sylvanas trusts him enough to hear the bad news plain and act on it immediately.
